mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts reflects broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 and Rank IC consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index registers a month-over-month increase exceeding 0.3%, rising inflation expectations compress real discount rates and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of triggered months, or the threshold signal shows zero predictive accuracy with Rank IC equal to 0 over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample forecasting models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal contracting manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, with the signal generating positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.
